title: Pour the cup again I thought love would be easy
text:
Pour the cup again I thought love would be easy
but every step has turned to steep stone
I asked the wind for the scent of her hair
it returned with blood-warm tears

The guide at the tavern said stain your prayer rug with wine
only then do travelers learn the road
Outside her gate the caravan bells kept crying
Tie your bundle again and move

Night was all whirlpool and black water
my voice could not reach the safe bright boats
I hunted my own name and found only disgrace
every mouth in the city wore my story

So I hold one sentence from the wise
When the heart receives what it asked for
let the world fall from your hands

title: The garden bird warned the rose at dawn
text:
The garden bird warned the rose at dawn
Many as beautiful as you have opened and vanished
She smiled and said fading does not wound me
what wounds me is a lover who speaks without tenderness

Sleep on the tavern threshold
love breathes most clearly in the dust there
If you want the jeweled cup of life
thread your eyes with a thousand tears

At night the wind crossed paradise alleys
I asked where is Jamshid's world-showing cup
where is the throne where love once sat
The breeze only sighed how long happiness sleeps

Love keeps its address hidden from ordinary speech
Come cupbearer the clever laughter is done
Patience and reason drowned long ago
in the sea my own longing made

title: From heaven's garden a west wind crossed my field
text:
From heaven's garden a west wind crossed my field
bring wine I said and let the day become a kingdom
Today even a beggar can sit like a king
under cloud-shadow tents in the ripening plain

April keeps saying spend what is in your hand
Do not pawn the present for imagined credit
Build your fortress with wine while your heart is warm
when it collapses the world will make bricks of your dust

Trust no sworn enemy no borrowed holiness
Do not judge the drunk in the court of appearances
no one has read the mark fate wrote on their own forehead

When I am gone walk behind me with one soft sigh
the sea of my errors may still open
into mercy

title: The rose opened and the nightingale went drunk with joy
text:
The rose opened and the nightingale went drunk with joy
Wine was announced to a thirsty world
Repentance looked like a mountain
one small cup split it cleanly in two

Pour for king and gatekeeper alike
at this table rank dissolves
When the second tavern door swings wide at midnight
all heads bend under one arch

No one reaches delight without passing through sorrow
No claim of Is or Is Not can hold
all that seems complete will empty into silence

Assaf's splendor is gone Solomon's wind-horses are gone
the speaking birds are gone
Aim your feather wisely
it flies one bowshot and falls to dust

Still if my reed can carry one true note
from mouth to mouth through the dark
that is thanks enough

title: Set my glass on fire with wine
text:
Set my glass on fire with wine
sing so the world turns toward desire
In the ring of the cup
I see her red cheek answering me

Do not stop these fleeting kindnesses
until she arrives cypress-tall and burning
Whoever keeps love's breath in the chest does not die
constancy writes itself into the world

When judgment comes what difference will remain
between lawful sips and forbidden ones
Tonight my friends' eyes shine intoxicated
I loosen the reins and go where they go

Wind if you pass my beloved's garden
ask why my name was erased
Tell them the day is near
when none of us will remember anyone

So I scatter tears like grain
to trap one bright bird of joy
My heart has crouched like a tulip in cold shade
I wait for fortune to enter the snare

title: Lady you hold my heart and still you do not listen
text:
Lady you hold my heart and still you do not listen
I spoke clearly the fault was never in the words
My drunk mind made a storm
but each wave called your name

I have no craft except surrender
no wealth except this worn-out longing
I traded reputation for one night near your door
and called the bargain mercy

What is wisdom without your face
what is prayer without your nearness
Every sermon dries in my mouth
until your memory turns to wine

If you pass and do not look
the whole city becomes exile
If you look once
the ruined house of me becomes spring

Do not ask how long I can endure
I have already spent my life answering
with breath
with ash
with song

title: Return and let courage enter this wounded heart
text:
Return and let courage enter this wounded heart
life has been waiting outside an empty door
Come back and touch my eyes
let sight wake inside this long darkness

Without you every hour is narrow iron
With you one glance makes even grief luminous
I have burned through patience like dry cedar
I have worn prayer thin as thread

Come so the dead blood moves again
come so this dust remembers rain
come so the tongue that broke in silence
can speak your name without breaking

I am not asking for paradise
only for your step in the courtyard
Only for your voice
and the room becomes endless

title: Everything hammered in the forge of life turns to nothing
text:
Everything hammered in the forge of life turns to nothing
fill the bowl because nothing else holds
Only one fire has ever lasted
the nearness of the Beloved

Crowns vanish arguments vanish markets vanish
the heart keeps one direction through the smoke
I have watched empires fold into dust
while one whisper outlived them all

Do not ask me for pious disguises
I have no use for polished hypocrisy
Give me the tavern where truth arrives barefoot
and every broken one is welcomed

If this world is a passing caravan
let me travel light
a clear mind
a singing chest
and one cup of astonishment

title: Do not throw blame at the drunkard's door
text:
Do not throw blame at the drunkard's door
keep your own page clean before you mark mine
I missed my path yes
but I walked it with an uncovered face

You call me ruined
I call this ruin honest
Better this cracked cup of longing
than a flawless life without love

If grace still visits anyone
let it visit the ones who fell in public
let it find us at daybreak
with ash on our sleeves and song in our mouths

I will not trade this ache for approval
I will not swap one real tear
for a thousand righteous smiles

title: What intoxication is this that suddenly gives me hope
text:
What intoxication is this that suddenly gives me hope
Who was the cupbearer what wine was that song
A hidden voice moved through the cords of music
and said your friend remembers you

Since then every grief has changed temperature
ice turning to water
water turning to light
I walk as if news has reached my bones

If this is illusion let me keep it
if this is mercy let me drown in it
I asked for one sign
the whole night answered in melody

Now even sorrow glows at the edges
as if dawn has already begun
inside the dark

title: Spring is here and the rose has risen from dust
text:
Spring is here and the rose has risen from dust
why do you still lie under dust
My eyes are rain-heavy clouds
I pour them over your grave

Even grief has seasons
but your silence does not move
I bring flowers and they fade
I bring your name and it burns

The garden is bright with return
only you remain absent
Teach me how to live in a world
that keeps blooming without you

title: The rose is nothing without the beloved's face
text:
The rose is nothing without the beloved's face
spring is not spring without laughter and wine
fields and soft winds and grain-heavy earth are pale
if her tulip-bright cheek is not there

What use are green paths or rivers of light
when the heart's sun is hidden
I have learned one law
beauty receives its meaning from presence

Bring me not gardens but one glance
not philosophy but one breath near me
In that nearness
the whole world flowers

title: My house became heaven when she lived in it
text:
My house became heaven when she lived in it
from head to foot she was grace in motion
The moon borrowed clarity from her face
wisdom softened inside her smile

Now every wall keeps her outline
every doorway waits for her step
the lamp still leans toward the place
where she used to sit and untie her hair

People ask what changed this room
I say one presence did
one breath did
one quiet turning of eyes did

If paradise has a map
it begins at the threshold she crossed
and ends nowhere

title: No one in the taverns is as mad as I am
text:
No one in the taverns is as mad as I am
My robe lies pawned for wine
my book lies pawned beside it
both holiness and shame spent in one night

My heart was made to mirror the king of hidden worlds
now it is clouded with street dust
I wipe and wipe and still your absence
writes itself across the glass

Do not ask me to return to polite restraint
I crossed that bridge and burned it for warmth
If madness means refusing a loveless life
then let this city call my name with laughter

I have one craft left
to keep a spark alive in ruin
until your face appears
and makes the mirror clear

title: The nights of separation are over at last
text:
The nights of separation are over at last
Who kept saying my star was cursed
that omen has broken

Autumn with all its yellow abundance has passed
the bitter ledger is closed
My breath no longer tastes of iron
my sleep no longer opens into exile

Every road now carries one answer
arrival
Every door repeats one word
home

Bring wine for the hour that returned to me
bring music for the wound that sealed
What was taken has not all returned
but enough has returned for praise

I stand in morning light and say
yes this is the same world
and no it is not

title: Singer scatter fresh notes over the night
text:
Singer scatter fresh notes over the night
again and again make the old heart new
Cupbearer I kiss the path of your feet
each step turns dust into festival

Do not ration joy
do not postpone the living hour
we are breath for a moment
then wind through an empty arch

So bring the red argument of wine
bring the quick mercy of melody
let laughter and tears sit at one table
and call each other by true names

If tomorrow comes
let it find us awake
with music on our tongues
and light in the cup

title: Count each passing hour as certain profit
text:
Count each passing hour as certain profit
what leaves the hand of fate does not return
Where is the cupbearer where is the jar
while spring still leans over the garden wall

Do not delay joy until certainty arrives
certainty never arrives
Only this breath this glance this poured light
is ours to hold

I have watched desire chase distant promises
and come back empty
I have watched one small present moment
open like a secret kingdom

So I choose the simple abundance
wine friendship a wind through leaves
and a heart that learns to say
this is enough

title: Where is my ruined life and where my noble name
text:
Where is my ruined life and where my noble name
Look at the road behind me and where it leads
I wanted holiness and found thirst
I wanted dignity and found smoke

Can piety and intoxication live in one chest
I have carried both and neither forgave me
The city reads my face like a warning
and still I go back to the cup

Because in that crimson edge of oblivion
I hear a truth no sermon gives
that we are fragile
that we are burning
that we are loved beyond deserving

If disgrace is my garment
let it be clean with honesty
I am tired of masks

title: From the street where my beloved lives send fragrance to me
text:
From the street where my beloved lives send fragrance to me
I am pale with waiting
I am ill with distance
one breath from that doorway would heal me

I would kiss the dust before her threshold
if that dust would carry my name inward
Nights pass like caravans without rest
bells of departure in every hour

Tell her I did not ask for justice
only remembrance
Tell her my heart has slept outside her gate
through winter through rumor through shame

If no answer comes
I will still sit here
facing her street
as if dawn depends on it

title: Not all the wealth of earth equals one bowed head in pain
text:
Not all the wealth of earth equals one bowed head in pain
If I sell my patched cloak for wine
what I receive is greater than what I lose

Land where my beloved lives holds me captive
willingly
I am chained by fragrance by memory by mercy
and I do not ask release

People count success in coins and titles
I count it in moments of nearness
in one look that strips fear from the chest
in one cup shared under night rain

If suffering is the tax of love
I have paid and will pay again
because love is the only kingdom
that does not vanish at dawn
